summary: Full E2E validation of Cyber Casino native games (Neon Slots + Neon Blackjack) plus Neon Roulette regression. Backend pytest 12/12 PASS. Frontend slots flow (balance fetch → chip select → spin → reels render → balance debit → paytable + provably-fair card visible) PASS. Frontend blackjack flow (balance fetch → deal → cards render → stand → settlement + outcome) PASS. Roulette page intact and untouched. Landing tiles all 3 navigate correctly.

minor.issue: Module-global _slot_seed_state mutated without a lock. Two concurrent /slots/spin requests can read the same server_seed+nonce before either rotates it, breaking the 'one secret per nonce' commit-reveal invariant. Single-process FastAPI + asyncio means this is rare but not impossible. Recommend asyncio.Lock around read+rotate, or persist the seed state in Mongo with findOneAndUpdate atomic increment.

issues: Below the 3 PLAYABLE tiles there is still a large 'Booting the Cyber Casino…' Unity WebGL / Spline hot-swap CTA section (Get Unity Hub, Or try Spline). Now that all 3 games are native + playable, this section is misleading copy and should either be removed or reworded to reflect that native games are live and Unity/Spline is just an optional future alternative.
data-testids cyber-casino-coming-soon, cyber-casino-unity-link, cyber-casino-spline-link still present

Provably-fair card shows next_hash and a 'Show last-spin proof' toggle but the actual server_seed/client_seed/nonce/hmac of the just-completed spin is one click away — consider auto-expanding after first spin so users see the reveal instantly (minor UX).

action_items: Hide or rework the 'Booting the Cyber Casino…' / Unity-WebGL / Spline placeholder section on /games/cyber-casino now that all 3 games are PLAYABLE — currently inconsistent with the tile state.

(Optional, low priority) Add asyncio.Lock around _slot_seed_state read+rotate in /app/backend/routes/cyber_casino.py to make concurrent spins strictly serialised — prevents a theoretical proof collision under concurrent load.
(Optional, polish) Auto-expand the just-revealed proof block on the slots page after a spin so users see the HMAC reveal without an extra click.
critical_code_review_comments: cyber_casino.py: _slot_seed_state is mutable module-level state with no lock — concurrency risk for the commit-reveal invariant.
cyber_casino.py: blackjack /action endpoint mutates session in-memory then writes back with $set — safe because each session_id is single-player, but worth noting the read-modify-write is not atomic.
cyber_casino.py: shoe is persisted to Mongo on every action (full 6-deck array). Fine for small scale but bloats audit/session storage; could persist only the deck index instead.
Tests look solid — hits paytable, commit, spin, verify, deal, action, double-rejection, audit, auth gating. 12/12 pass.
test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (auto-provisioned demo user, 2000 Vibez Coins start)
seed_data_creation: None — each test demo-logs a fresh user. Audit collection received real entries during test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Cyber Casino native game flows verified end-to-end. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_cyber_casino.py is 12/12 green and uses fixtures from /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env). Frontend data-testids: slots-{back-btn,balance-card,balance,reels,reel-0/1/2,chip-10/25/50/100/250/500,spin-btn,paytable,provably-fair}; bj-{back-btn,balance-card,balance,dealer-cards,player-cards,chip-row,chip-25/50/100/250/500/1000,deal-btn,hit-btn,stand-btn,double-btn}; cyber-casino-tile-{roulette,slots,blackjack}. Neon Roulette is untouched and still works (local-only state, intentional per founder). Only outstanding item is the cosmetic landing-page Unity placeholder block that lingers below the playable tile grid.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. All issues raised are cosmetic (landing-page placeholder copy) or hardening recommendations (asyncio lock on slot seed).
